expectations.text: Covers CRP, image, JS, font areas
expectations.evidence: Output does not organize findings by CRP, image, JS, and font web performance areas. Instead it analyzes the SKILL.md files for code-level anti-patterns (getComputedStyle in loop, sequential skill execution, shell command inefficiency, redundant git diff). No section addresses image optimization, font optimization, or Critical Rendering Path as web performance categories.

Output lists 6 code review findings (unbounded DOM query, repeated file reads, sequential execution, shell inefficiency, redundant git diff, no issues in JSON). These are not PERF checklist items or equivalent web performance audit items -- they are code quality findings about the skill definition files themselves.
Each of the 6 findings has a severity level, location, description of the anti-pattern, and a recommendation. The summary table provides finding number, description, severity, and file for each item.

suggestions: Without the skill, the agent interpreted 'performance anti-patterns' as general code performance issues rather than web performance checklist areas (CRP, image, JS, font). The skill provides the structured PERF-01 to PERF-11 framework that guides the review toward web-specific performance areas.
eval_feedback.overall: Without-skill run produced a thoughtful code review but missed the expected web performance audit structure. It analyzed the skill definition files for runtime efficiency issues rather than applying a web performance checklist with CRP, image, JS, and font categories. This demonstrates the value of the skill in guiding the agent toward the intended review framework.
